chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No.2115 of 2025 Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz ul Hassan Shah Applicant:- Muhammad Ghaffar through Mr. Mallag Assa Dashti, Advocate. Respondent:- The State through Mr. Zohaib Ahmed, Special Prosecutor custom along with I.O. Mehran Khan Shaikh. Date of hearing:- 10.02.2026 =============== MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant claims to be a delivery clerk working in a Gateway Company at Jinnah International Airport, Karachi. Allegedly, one parcel came from Nigeria containing 9.500 Kgs of Hashish/Weed and the applicant went to International Mail Office to get the said parcel cleared. The parcel on a tip-off was already under observation by the Custom Department and hence they arrested the applicant and checked his mobile phone which revealed that he was in constant contact with the main accused, who had assigned him responsibility of getting the parcel cleared. 2. The defence of the applicant for bail, argued by his counsel, is that he is a delivery clerk and it is his duty to go to International Mail Office on behalf of the company to get the parcels cleared. His plea for bail has been opposed by Special Prosecutor Custom and I.O. 3. It appears prima facie that applicant did not act under instructions from the company but on his own for collecting the suspicious parcel which prima facie makes him an accused in this case. That is further strengthened from the fact that he was found in constant touch with the main accused on phone which has not been explained, because the delivery clerk otherwise working in the company could not be in a direct contact with the client.

It appears prima facie that applicant did not act under instructions from the company but on his own for collecting the suspicious parcel which prima facie makes him an accused in this case. That is further strengthened from the fact that he was found in constant touch with the main accused on phone which has not been explained, because the delivery clerk otherwise working in the company could not be in a direct contact with the client. Therefore, we do not find the applicant entitled to concession of bail and hence dismiss this bail application. The Bail Application is disposed of accordingly. The observations herein above are tentative in nature and shall not affect the case of either party on merits before the trial Court. JUDGE JUDGE HANIF
